signals.title: USD/JPY — Geopolitical Shock + Carry + Vol
signals.text: USD/JPY at 159.62 — Tehran halting nuclear ceasefire talks and a blowout US ISM Manufacturing print (highest since 2022) drive a dual-engine bid for the dollar. Despite the geopolitical tension, the +289bp SOFR-TONA carry advantage keeps the pair bid as VIX remains at 16.1 (normal volatility). JPY COT LF net short -86,249 (31.6% long) indicates a crowded carry trade vulnerable to intervention, though 25d RR at -1.06 shows the options market is already paying a moderate premium for JPY protection. 160.00 remains the critical level for BoJ verbal warnings or direct intervention.

signals.title: NZD/USD — COT vs OIS Divergence + Risk-Off
signals.text: NZD/USD at 0.5936 — COT LF net short -13,767 (specs fading the RBNZ) vs a hawkish RBNZ market bias (Hike priced for next meeting). The pair fell 1.00% today as safe-haven USD demand eclipsed a positive China PMI surprise. With NZD real rates at -0.83% (most accommodative in G8), the currency lacks structural support during risk-off shifts. If RBNZ signals a hike on July 8, a crowded spec short unwind targets 0.6050; otherwise, the path of least resistance remains lower toward 0.5900.

signals.title: USD/CHF — Volatility Premium + Carry
signals.text: USD/CHF at 0.7866 — IV at 17.5% vs HV30 at 6.4% generates a massive +11.1% Vol Risk Premium (VRP), signaling extreme institutional demand for protection despite neutral speculative positioning (COT LF net short -5,651). The +375bp Fed-SNB carry advantage structurally favors USD, but the surge in VRP suggests the market is pricing a tail-risk event or a sharp SNB policy shift. Carry remains the dominant driver unless VIX crosses 25.

signals.title: AUD/USD — Positioning + Vol + Retail Divergence
signals.text: AUD/USD at 0.7163 — COT LF net long +58,041 (76.1% long) vs retail 75% short creates a sharp institutional-retail divergence. While RBA at 4.35% provides a -60bp carry advantage over the Fed, the high IV (16.0%) and +7.9% VRP indicate the market is hedging against a breakdown of the spec long position. Retail is aggressively fading the institutional trend; a break below 0.7100 would likely trigger a massive spec liquidation.

signals.title: DXY — Correlation Break + Data Strength
signals.text: DXY at 99.18 — Correlation vs SPX at -0.602 (z=+1.71σ) indicates a significant deviation from the 252-day norm of -0.315, signaling USD funding stress as the dollar and equities move in tighter inverse lockstep. This is supported by a Strong Positive Economic Surprise Index (CESI net +21), confirming that US macro outperformance is the primary driver of the current USD bid. Watch for a reversal in the bear flattener regime (2s10s at +48.5bp) to signal a peak in USD momentum.
